text: After inspection of the pleural surface to confirm the absence of metastasis, the lung was retracted posteriorly and the mediastinal pleura was opened widely along the anterior hilum extending the dissection posteriorly.
The superior pulmonary vein was encircled with a vessel loop and divided with a 35 mm vascular stapler.
The removal of the interlobal lymphatic tissue might help in the identification of the division of the upper lobe and the intermedius bronchus.
The interlobal artery was reached via the fissure using a combination of bipolar energy and blunt dissection.
Completion of the anterior and posterior fissure was performed using a 60 mm endostepler.

The division of the pulmonary ligament as well as the subcarinal lymph node dissection before the bronchial sleeve procedure can also facilitate the lung mobility and avoid traction and manipulation of the anastomosis.
The lobe is then pulled anteriorly and upward in order to expose the upper lobe bronchus.
The bronchus was cut using a monopolar robotic scissor starting from the anterior wall of the right main bronchus.
The specimen was then removed through the utility incision using an endo bag.
After evaluation of the bronchial stumps by prosencection, an end-to-end anastomosis was carried out.

We perform the bronchial anastomosis using two running sutures with PDS stratafix spiral knotless 3-0, starting from the caudal corner of the pars metabranacea and progressing on the cartilaginous portion.
Both sutures were then tied through a double knot.
The final pathological report confirmed a squamous cell carcinoma of the lung and the final pathological stage was PT2AN0 .
